cards.definition: The concentration of Indofil M-45 fungicide used to treat Cercospora (brown-eye-spot) disease in nursery seedlings.
cards.analogy: A calibrated dose for a specific nursery disease, not a general-purpose fungicide splash.
cards.why_it_matters: A treatable, well-defined nursery disease — worth distinguishing from other seedling problems that need a different fungicide entirely.
cards.cloze: Cercospora (brown-eye-spot) disease in nursery seedlings is treated by spraying Indofil M-45 at ___ g/litre of water.

cards.definition: The concentration of Tilt (propiconazole) used to treat Myrothecium roridum, a distinct nursery fungal disease from Cercospora.
cards.analogy: A different fungicide at a different dose for a different pathogen — not interchangeable with the Cercospora treatment.
cards.why_it_matters: Mixing up this dose with Cercospora's would mean the wrong chemical and wrong strength for whichever disease is actually present.
cards.cloze: Myrothecium roridum fungal disease in the nursery is treated with Tilt (propiconazole) at ___ ml per litre of water.
